offer: Full-service digital solutions agency, AI-and-automation-first: AI automation, chatbot development, lead generation systems, digital marketing (ads, social media, SEO), CRM setup and automation, custom automation, web design/development, and other digital services.
requested_industry_rule: The industry requested for a run DEFINES the target market for that run and is treated as in-scope, even if it is not in default_target_market. Score those leads on QUALITY and fit signals — never cap or reject a lead merely because its industry isn't in the default list. Example: a run requesting 'Real Estate' should return real-estate business owners scored on quality, not zero.
role_rule: Only the founder/owner counts as a lead. Employees, marketing managers, or generic company pages are not leads. This is mandatory on every run regardless of industry.
default_target_market: Agency owners: marketing, creative, design, PR, media, branding, content, video, web design agencies
Consultants (broad business sense): strategy, management, sales, finance, investment, HR, industry-specific consultancies
default_target_market_note: Used only when NO specific industry is requested for the run. When a run names an industry, that industry is the target instead.
always_exclude: Chains and multi-location networks and their branch offices (fitness chains, franchise branches, retail/bank chains) — the local Geschäftsführer is an employee-executive, not a founder/owner. Applies to EVERY run and is never overridden by the requested industry.
Businesses that are inactive, in liquidation, or deleted per registry

Direct competitors: businesses whose PRIMARY offering is the same AI-automation / digital-agency service we sell. A requested target industry is a potential client, not a competitor — only exclude a lead as a competitor if selling to them makes no sense because they already do exactly what we do.
hard_disqualifiers: Founder / owner not identifiable (mandatory — no founder, not a lead)
No phone number (phone is the primary channel: German law restricts cold email, calls come first). A missing/unverified email is OK; a missing phone is a disqualifier.

firmographics.revenue_note: Solo to lower-mid-market. Missing revenue/employee data is NOT disqualifying — score on available signals and state confidence.

positive_signals: Visible service pages and portfolio/case studies
Founder with a personal brand (visible on site, LinkedIn, talks)
Visible manual processes / dated website tech = automation upside
negative_signals: Website 'coming soon' or last updated years ago
No Impressum (for German companies — legally required, its absence is a red flag)
Aggregator-only presence (only Yelp/Instagram, no own site)

scoring.rules: Any hard disqualifier or always_exclude match = score below threshold (0 for founder/website/phone failures, <=20 for always_exclude). Otherwise start from the QUALITY of the lead: an owner-led business in the requested industry, with a phone and a clean site, starts around 70; adjust up/down with the positive/negative signals and firmographics. Do NOT lower a score just because the industry isn't in default_target_market when that industry was explicitly requested.

per_run_overrides: Merge any run-specific criteria the requester states (e.g. 'website agencies, ~100k/month revenue, ~5 employees') over this file for that run only.
